name: Date-Nut Pumpkin Pie
recipeIngredient: Sweetened whipped cream or Whipped topping
recipeInstructions:
1. Roll and press crust into a 9-inch pie plate. Do not bake. Reserve dough scraps for cut-outs if desired. Heat oven to 450°F.
2. Combine dates and water in a medium saucepan. Cook on medium heat until the mixture comes to a boil and dates have softened. Add 1/3 cup brown sugar and butter. Stir to blend. Remove from heat. Stir in nuts and cinnamon. Cool while preparing filling.
3. Beat eggs lightly in a medium bowl. Add pumpkin, granulated and packed brown sugars, evaporated milk, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, salt, and ground cloves. Stir to blend.
4. Spoon the date-nut mixture evenly into the unbaked pie crust. Pour in the pumpkin filling. Bake at 450°F for 10 minutes. Reduce oven to 350°F and continue baking for 35 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool to room temperature before garnishing.
5. Pipe or spoon sweetened whipped cream around the outer edges of the pie before serving. Refrigerate leftover pie.
